name: qdrant-minimize-latency
prompt: Our recommendation API does a single vector search per request and p99 has sat around 250ms since launch — product wants it under 60ms. Throughput isn't the issue; even at low traffic one query is slow, and the collection fits comfortably in RAM on a large node. What can we tune to make individual queries faster?

rubric.text: Increases segment count toward CPU core count (e.g. default_segment_number ~16) for lower per-query latency
Reduces hnsw_ef at query time to trade some recall for speed
Keeps HNSW and quantized vectors in RAM (always_ram=true)
Recommends local NVMe / avoiding network-attached storage
Limits background optimization CPU (optimizer_cpu_budget) or schedules indexing off-peak
Applies throughput tuning (fewer/larger segments) — the opposite direction from latency
Treats it as an incident to diagnose or recommends adding nodes for QPS
